ddls:
CREATE TABLE "mux_settings" (
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each MUX setting */,
  "bit_range" TEXT /* Bit range associated with the MUX setting */,
  "field_name" TEXT /* Name of the MUX field */,
  "operation_type" TEXT /* Type of operation allowed (read/write) */,
  "reset_value" TEXT /* Default reset value for the MUX setting */,
  "description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the MUX setting's function */,
  "created_by" INTEGER /* Reference to the engineer who created the setting */,
  "created_date" TEXT /* Date when the setting was created */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* Reference to the engineer who last updated the setting */,
  "updated_date" TEXT /* Date when the setting was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("setting_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "mux_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each MUX version */,
  "version_name" TEXT /* Name of the MUX version */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date when the MUX version was released */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the MUX version */,
  "end_of_life_date" TEXT /* Date when the MUX version reached end-of-life */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_lines" (
  "product_line_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product line */,
  "product_name" TEXT /* Name of the product */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the MUX version used in the product line */,
  "release_date" TEXT /* Date when the product line was released */,
  "product_manager_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the product manager responsible for the product line */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("product_line_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "configuration_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each configuration change */,
  "product_line_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the product line where the change occurred */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the MUX version involved in the change */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date when the configuration change was made */,
  "change_description" TEXT /* Description of the configuration change */,
  "changed_by" INTEGER /* Reference to the engineer who made the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "engineers" (
  "engineer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each engineer */,
  "engineer_name" TEXT /* Full name of the engineer */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the engineer */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the engineer (e.g., junior engineer, senior engineer) */,
  "supervisor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the supervisor of the engineer */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("engineer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "engineer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the engineer who accessed the setting */,
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the MUX setting accessed */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the setting was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "quality_assurance" (
  "qa_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality assurance test */,
  "product_line_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the product line tested */,
  "test_date" TEXT /* Date when the quality assurance test was conducted */,
  "test_result" TEXT /* Result of the quality assurance test */,
  "comments" TEXT /* Additional comments or notes about the test */,
  "tested_by" INTEGER /* Reference to the engineer who conducted the test */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("qa_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "issue_tracking" (
  "issue_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each issue or defect */,
  "product_line_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the product line where the issue was found */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the issue or defect */,
  "reported_date" TEXT /* Date when the issue was reported */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the issue (e.g., open, closed, resolved) */,
  "reported_by" INTEGER /* Reference to the engineer who reported the issue */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("issue_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "documents" (
  "doc_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each document */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the MUX version the document is associated with */,
  "file_path" TEXT /* File path to the document */,
  "file_size" REAL /* Size of the document file in MB */,
  "upload_date" TEXT /* Date the document was uploaded */,
  "uploaded_by" INTEGER /* Reference to the engineer who uploaded the document */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("doc_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user permission */,
  "engineer_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the engineer who has the permission */,
  "product_line_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the product line the permission applies to */,
  "access_level" TEXT /* Level of access granted (e.g., read-only, edit, admin) */,
  "granted_date" TEXT /* Date when the permission was granted */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "product_line_configurations" (
  "config_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each product line configuration */,
  "product_line_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the product line this configuration applies to */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the MUX version used in this configuration */,
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the MUX setting being configured */,
  "configured_value" TEXT /* Configured value for the MUX setting */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("config_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "change_requests" (
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change request */,
  "product_line_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the product line this change request applies to */,
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the MUX version involved in this change request */,
  "setting_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the MUX setting being changed */,
  "requested_change" TEXT /* Description of the requested change */,
  "requested_by" INTEGER /* Reference to the engineer who requested the change */,
  "request_date" TEXT /* Date when the change was requested */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("request_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "change_request_status" (
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status update */,
  "request_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the change request being tracked */,
  "status_date" TEXT /* Date when the status was updated */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current status of the change request (e.g., pending, approved, rejected) */,
  "updated_by" INTEGER /* Reference to the engineer who updated the status */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("status_id")
)
